activity:
6:30 AM – Morning Mindfulness and Clinical Review  
The healthcare professional begins the day with 30 minutes of guided mindfulness meditation and breathwork to center themselves before the emotional intensity of patient care. They then review overnight electronic health records (EHR) for high-risk patients on opioid agonist therapy (e.g., methadone or buprenorphine), noting any signs of withdrawal, non-adherence, or potential relapse triggers. They cross-reference recent urine toxicology screens and prescription drug monitoring program (PDMP) data to identify red flags such as doctor shopping or overlapping prescriptions.
